top_stories.title: Hackers bypass SonicWall VPN MFA due to incomplete patching
top_stories.summary: Threat actors bypassed multi-factor authentication on SonicWall Gen6 SSL-VPN appliances by brute-forcing VPN credentials, enabling ransomware deployment. The MFA bypass was possible due to incomplete patching on the affected SonicWall devices.

top_stories.title: GitHub, Grafana Labs breaches traced back to TanStack supply chain compromise
top_stories.summary: A malicious version of the Nx Console VS Code extension, part of a TanStack supply chain compromise by threat group TeamPCP, was used to steal developer credentials and secrets from GitHub and Grafana Labs. The stolen credentials enabled attackers to move through CI/CD pipelines and exfiltrate approximately 3,800 of GitHub's private code repositories.

top_stories.title: The New Phishing Click: How OAuth Consent Bypasses MFA
top_stories.summary: A phishing-as-a-service platform called EvilTokens compromised over 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries within five weeks of launching in February 2026. The attack exploits OAuth device code flow, tricking users into completing legitimate MFA challenges while unknowingly granting attackers persistent OAuth tokens, effectively bypassing MFA protections.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Self-Service Password Reset abused in Azure data theft attacks
top_stories.summary: A threat actor is actively abusing Microsoft Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) and other legitimate Azure administration features to steal data from Microsoft 365 and Azure production environments. The attacks leverage trusted applications and built-in identity management functions to carry out data exfiltration, implicating core IAM features in the attack chain.

top_stories.title: How Storm-2949 turned a compromised identity into a cloud-wide breach
top_stories.summary: A threat actor tracked as Storm-2949 executed a cloud-wide breach by leveraging compromised credentials and moving laterally through cloud systems without deploying malware. The attack demonstrates how identity compromise alone can enable large-scale data theft by exploiting trusted systems and identities.

news.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
news.summary: New Omdia research examines how AI agent identity management is creating distinct budget dynamics compared to traditional IAM projects. The research highlights the growing need to manage, secure, and govern AI agent identities as these projects proliferate across enterprises.

news.title: Microsoft Defender vulnerabilities exploited in the wild (CVE-2026-41091, CVE-2026-45498)
news.summary: Two Microsoft Defender vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-41091 and CVE-2026-45498) are being actively exploited in the wild, with CISA adding them to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. CVE-2026-41091 enables local privilege elevation to SYSTEM level via improper link resolution in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine, while CVE-2026-45498 can trigger a denial-of-service condition.

news.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
news.summary: Microsoft has disclosed two actively exploited vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender, including a privilege escalation flaw (CVE-2026-41091, CVSS 7.8) that could allow attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges. The vulnerabilities involve improper link resolution before file access and a denial-of-service flaw, both confirmed as exploited in the wild.

news.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
news.summary: An analysis examines how a cached AWS access key on a single Windows machine created an attack path to approximately 98% of entities in a company's cloud environment. The scenario illustrates how standard credential caching behavior, rather than misconfiguration, can create critical identity-based lateral movement risks in cloud infrastructure.

news.title: Identity Alone Isn't Enough: Why Device Security Has to Share the Load
news.summary: Specops Software published a piece arguing that identity verification alone is insufficient for Zero Trust security due to threats like stolen session tokens and compromised devices. The article advocates for continuous device verification as a necessary complement to identity checks in Zero Trust strategies.

news.title: Agent AI is Coming. Are You Ready?
news.summary: Orchid Security released its "Identity Gap: Snapshot 2026" report on May 19th, 2026, examining unmanaged identity elements in enterprise environments. The report found that "identity dark matter" — unseen and unmanaged identity components — now accounts for 57% of enterprise identity surface area, outpacing visible managed identities at 43%, with the trend coinciding with growing enterprise adoption of Agent AI.

news.title: Security Hub Extended expands to 21 curated partner solutions across 9 categories
news.summary: AWS expanded its Security Hub Extended plan to include 21 curated partner solutions across 9 security categories. CyberArk is listed as one of the included partners under the identity category.

news.title: Provision IAM Announces Strategic Investments from Georgia's Own and InTouch Credit Unions
news.summary: Provision IAM announced strategic investments from Georgia's Own Credit Union and InTouch Credit Union, expanding its Customer Advisory Board. The partnerships align Provision IAM with the credit union sector as part of its broader industry growth strategy.

news.title: Okta (OKTA) Could Raise Its Fiscal 2027 Guidance Amid Improved Demand And Execution, Says Barclays
news.summary: Barclays analysts suggested Okta could raise its fiscal 2027 guidance based on improved demand and execution. The commentary positions Okta as a top cloud stock pick with a noted short float of 5.03% and 21.50% upside potential.

news.title: Keeper Security Launches ServiceNow Workflow Integration for Governed, Self-Service Vault Management
news.summary: Keeper Security has launched a new integration with ServiceNow that brings Keeper Vault operations directly into ServiceNow's Service Catalog and Flow Designer. The integration covers access requests, record storage, and endpoint privilege management approvals within ServiceNow workflows.

news.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
news.summary: CISA inadvertently exposed secrets and credentials in a GitHub repository that was publicly accessible since November 2025. The repository, ironically named "Private-CISA," contained sensitive information that should have been kept confidential.

news.title: Compromised Nx Console 18.95.0 Targeted VS Code Developers with Credential Stealer
news.summary: A compromised version of the Nx Console VS Code extension (version 18.95.0) was published to the Microsoft Visual Studio Code Marketplace, targeting developers with a credential stealer. The malicious extension, with over 2.2 million installations, was designed to steal credentials from users of VS Code, Cursor, and JetBrains editors.

news.title: Popular GitHub Action Tags Redirected to Imposter Commit to Steal CI/CD Credentials
news.summary: Threat actors compromised the GitHub Actions workflow "actions-cool/issues-helper" by redirecting all existing repository tags to a malicious imposter commit. The attack is designed to harvest and exfiltrate sensitive CI/CD credentials, including secrets and tokens used in automated pipeline environments.

news.title: CIRT insights: How to help prevent unauthorized account removals from AWS Organizations
news.summary: The AWS Customer Incident Response Team published insights on preventing unauthorized account removals from AWS Organizations, based on patterns observed during active security incident responses. The guidance addresses threat actor tactics that exploit specific customer configurations to remove accounts from AWS Organizations, which has direct implications for cloud access governance and privilege management.

news.title: Azure Files Entra-Only identities: Advancing cloud-native identity and security
news.summary: Microsoft has announced the general availability of Entra-Only identities for Azure Files SMB, enabling identity-based access to SMB file shares using cloud-native Microsoft Entra ID authentication. This eliminates the previous requirement for on-premises Active Directory or hybrid identity configurations when accessing Azure Files over SMB.

news.title: EMA Research Finds Legacy IAM Systems Are Slowing AI Adoption in Financial Sector
news.summary: EMA Research, commissioned by Ory Corp., surveyed 271 IT and security professionals and found that legacy IAM systems are impeding AI adoption in the financial and insurance sectors. Financial institutions show significantly lower full-scale AI production deployment rates (29.7%) compared to the broader market (40.6%), with IAM infrastructure identified as a key bottleneck.

news.title: Automation Anywhere Collaborates with Cisco, NVIDIA, Okta, and OpenAI, Launching EnterpriseClaw to Run Next-Generation AI Agents Inside Enterprise Systems
news.summary: Automation Anywhere announced a collaboration with Cisco, NVIDIA, Okta, and OpenAI to launch EnterpriseClaw, a platform for running AI agents across enterprise environments. Okta is listed as one of several partners, but the announcement is primarily focused on AI agent automation rather than identity or access management capabilities.

news.title: Critical Microsoft Vulnerabilities Doubled: From Exposure to Escalation
news.summary: BeyondTrust's analysis reveals that critical Microsoft vulnerabilities doubled in 2025 compared to the prior year, while the overall vulnerability count remained steady. The report highlights a shift in attacker focus toward privilege escalation and identity abuse as primary exploitation vectors.

news.title: MajorKey Technologies Selected to Deliver DDIL Identity Capabilities at the Tactical Edge
news.summary: MajorKey Technologies has been selected by a U.S. Department of Defense agency to deliver its NomadID solution for identity capabilities in disconnected, denied, intermittent, and limited (DDIL) environments. NomadID is designed to provide continuous, secure access management at the tactical edge where traditional connectivity-dependent identity systems cannot function.

news.title: Two-Thirds of Nonhuman Accounts Are Unseen and Unmanaged, According to Orchid Security's Identity Gap Report
news.summary: Orchid Security released its Identity Gap Report finding that two-thirds of nonhuman accounts in enterprises are unseen and unmanaged. The research highlights what the company calls "identity dark matter," linking unmanaged nonhuman identities to weaknesses in enterprise identity posture and AI agent readiness.

news.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
news.summary: A set of vulnerabilities dubbed "Claw Chain" were discovered in the OpenClaw AI agent framework, now patched, that allowed attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence. The flaws affected deployments of the rapidly growing framework, exposing credential theft and privilege escalation risks.

news.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
news.summary: Three separate supply chain attacks targeting npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub occurred within a 48-hour window, all focused on stealing secrets from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines. The stolen credentials included API keys, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and authentication tokens from developer workstations.

news.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
news.summary: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, and n8n released security patches addressing vulnerabilities including authentication bypass and privilege escalation flaws. The most critical issue is CVE-2026-8043 (CVSS 9.6) affecting Ivanti Xtraction, which could enable information disclosure or client-side attacks.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files describing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes alongside the sensitive credentials.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted to conducting SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that enabled the group to compromise at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to silently compromise user access.
